document.title: Risk Management Report for B Series

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports documentation of risk management activities, risk control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information for the medical device.
keyPoints: The document applies to Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
Applicable regulations listed are Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
The device is classified as IIa and has an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Before risk control, 43 risks were identified: 2 acceptable and 41 unacceptable.
After risk control, all 43 identified residual risks were evaluated in the acceptable region.

Production and post-production information is to be collected and reviewed, including customer satisfaction, complaints, inspection results, design changes, standards monitoring, and adverse event reporting if necessary.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: European Union
entities.evidence: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

entities.name: Republic of Korea
entities.evidence: Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: If the plan changes during the life cycle of the medical device, a record of the changes shall be maintained in the risk management file.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4; Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Overall residual risk
concepts.evidence: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the residual risks within the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector are reduced in the acceptable region

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: Refer to A-TCF-10-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator.

evidence.claim: The document is a Risk Management Report for B Series.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Report for B Series
evidence.pageTarget: Risk Management Report for B Series

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The covered models are B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report revision is Rev.0 and the revision date is Oct/18/2024.
evidence.pageTarget: Risk Management Report for B Series

evidence.claim: The prepared, reviewed, and approved dates are Oct 18, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by R&D team Kim, Myeong seop Oct 18, 2024; Reviewed by R&D Manager Lee, Dong ho Oct 18, 2024; Approved by Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Oct 18, 2024
evidence.pageTarget: Risk Management Report for B Series

evidence.claim: The document lists European Union and Republic of Korea regulations as applicable national and regional regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017; Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 43 risks were identified, with 2 acceptable and 41 unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 43 risks were identified and estimated. Acceptable region: 2 ea Unacceptable region: 41 ea

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 43 identified residual risks were in the acceptable region.
All identified residual risks after risk control were distributed in an acceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

evidence.claim: Risk control measures included labeling and user manual, certified battery, IEC 60601-1 testing, different cable types, IEC 62304 software process, radio testing, and certified RF module.
evidence.evidenceText: As control measures for the identified risks, providing label and user manual, using certified battery, testing the device to meet IEC 60601-1, designing each cable type differently, establishing software development process in accordance with IEC 62304, radio-related testing, and using certified RF module, etc. were implemented and verified.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information includes customer satisfaction surveys, complaint statistics, inspection results, measuring device monitoring, design changes, standards monitoring, market information, process monitoring, and purchaser assessment monitoring.
evidence.evidenceText: Production and post-production information Collection and review activities in production and post-production phase

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for collecting, reviewing, and managing quality problems in production and post-production phase.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase

evidence.claim: Adverse event reporting is required if necessary according to the seriousness of collected information.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text lists European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea; no FDA authority text is present.
Document does not provide a single explicit document number in the title field, though referenced path and internal references indicate A-TCF-10-104.
OCR or source quality issues are present, including malformed wording such as 'H&abyz', 'S / W', and apparent standard typo 'EN 15322-1' where the normative references list EN 15223-1:2021.
The source states essential performance functions, but later states the device use does not depend on essential performance because it is not life-supporting; this may require regulatory review.
